sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_date DATE);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date) VALUES (1, DATE '1990-01-01'), (2, DATE '1995-06-15'), (3, DATE '2000-12-31'), (4, NULL);
SELECT id, decade(event_date) AS date_decade FROM test_dates WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL) HAVING ~decade(event_date) IS NULL;
CREATE TABLE test_bitstrings (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, bit_data BITSTRING);
INSERT INTO test_bitstrings (id, bit_data) VALUES (1, '101'::BITSTRING), (2, '110'::BITSTRING), (3, NULL);

SELECT t1.id, t1.event_date, t2.bit_data, decade(t1.event_date) + ~CAST(t2.bit_data AS INTEGER) AS complex_calc FROM test_dates t1 JOIN test_bitstrings t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.event_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, decade(event_date) AS decade_val, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY decade(event_date)) AS decade_count FROM test_dates GROUP BY decade_val, id HAVING ~decade_val IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE temp_join (join_id INTEGER, ref_date DATE);
INSERT INTO temp_join (join_id, ref_date) VALUES (1, DATE '2020-01-01'), (2, DATE '2025-01-01'), (3, NULL);
SELECT t1.id, t1.event_date, t2.ref_date FROM test_dates t1 JOIN temp_join t2 ON t1.id = t2.join_id WHERE decade(t1.event_date) = ~decade(t2.ref_date) AND t2.ref_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, decade(event_date) AS decade_val FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NULL OR (? IS NOT NULL AND decade(event_date) IN (1, 2, 3)) ORDER BY ~decade_val DESC;
